channel.title: The Grant Desk - Tech & Infrastructure, US
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Tech & Infrastructure, US.

item.title: TechCongress: Congressional Innovation Fellowship 2027 (Senior and Early-Career)
item.description:
Organisation: TechCongress

Location: Washington, DC, United States. Full-time, in-person; remote participation is not possible. Nonpartisan: fellows serve in both Democratic and Republican offices.

Award: Senior (mid-career) Fellows: USD 100,600/year stipend. Early-career Fellows: USD 78,000 annual-equivalent stipend (USD 6,500/month). Plus health insurance supplement up to USD 425/month, up to USD 2,000 relocation to DC, up to USD 2,000 first-month short-term housing reimbursement, up to USD 2,000 official travel, and a USD 500 professional-attire allowance. No application fee.

Duration: Senior Fellows serve January to December 2027 (optional one-month extension). Early-career Fellows serve January to October 2027.

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

TechCongress places technologists in the US Congress to build technology capacity among Members and Congressional staff, where fewer than 3% of legislative staff have a technical background. For the January 2027 cohort it is recruiting up to twenty fellows (10 for Democratic offices, 10 for Republican offices) across two tiers: the Senior Congressional Innovation Fellowship (mid-career, at least eight years of work or postgraduate study) and the Congressional Innovation Fellowship (early-career, two to six years of experience, including a recently finished or projected-by-January-2027 graduate technical degree such as a Masters or PhD; applicants with seven years qualify for an intermediate tier). Fellows choose a placement with a Member of Congress or a Congressional Committee, report to a senior staffer (such as a Chief of Staff or Staff Director), and do work similar to other Congressional staff: researching tech policy (cyber and election security, data and biometric privacy, AI policy, autonomous vehicles, health IT, encryption, disinformation), educating Members and staff, writing legislation, preparing Committee hearings, markups and investigations, and building coalitions. No prior government experience is required; the fellowship is first and foremost an educational experience. This year TechCongress also seeks at least two fellows from non-traditional verticals: climate (grid modernization, clean energy, NEPA reform), government innovation (govtech and digital benefits delivery, civil service reform), and science, research and innovation (NSF, NIH, DoE, FDA reform, clinical trials). Technical degrees or experience in engineering, development, journalism, scientific, legal or government roles are encouraged. TechCongress is a nonpartisan nonprofit incubated at the Open Technology Institute at New America; since 2016 it has sent 140 fellows to Congress. Equal opportunity employer. Application deadline: 26 June 2026, 5:59am (applicant's local timezone). Contact: aleena@techcongress.io. Apply via the official application form.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Exygy: Senior Product Designer (Bloom, temporary)
item.description:
Organisation: Exygy

Location: Remote, United States

Award: Paid role: $97,750-$132,250 per year for a temporary engagement. No application fee.

Duration: Temporary engagement Q3 2026 to Q1 2027

Deadline: Closed 08 Jun 2026

Exygy is hiring a temporary Senior Product Designer (UX/UI) to work on Bloom, its affordable-housing platform. Remote within the United States. Compensation: $97,750-$132,250 per year. Engagement runs Q3 2026 to Q1 2027. NOTE: this is a job, not a grant or fellowship. Application deadline Monday, 8 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Robin Hood (via Blue Ridge Labs venture studio)

Location: United States (Phase 1 virtual; Phase 2 in-person at BRL Lab in Brooklyn, NYC)

Award: $20,000 stipend in Phase 2 to support MVP development. No equity terms mentioned. Includes weekly mentorship and coaching, workshops on product, growth and fundraising, co-working space at BRL Lab Brooklyn, access to the Design Insights Group (DIG) community for user testing, and network introductions across tech, philanthropy and policy.

Duration: Phase 1 (Explore & Test): 6 to 8 weeks, June to July 2026, virtual-first. Phase 2 (Build MVP): 10 to 12 weeks, September to November 2026, in-person Brooklyn.

Deadline: Closed 03 May 2026

Founder fellowship for early-stage or new founders tackling problems in Robin Hood's five economic-mobility focus areas: Education, Financial Empowerment, Health, Housing, and Hunger & Jobs. Applicants need lived or learned experience with the challenges they propose to address. No prototype or co-founder required at application. Approximately 25 founders advance from Phase 1 to Phase 2. Open to US applicants only; no relocation expected for Phase 1, but Phase 2 requires in-person time at the BRL Lab in Brooklyn. Apply via the Airtable application form linked from the Robin Hood page; submit an idea summary and background. Fellowship is fully funded by Robin Hood (no application fee, no equity stake disclosed). Note: this is a startup/entrepreneur-style fellowship rather than an arts/research fellowship; applicants should be building venture-track ideas tied to economic mobility outcomes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pace Research Fellowship Summer 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Pace

Location: Pace HQ, New York City, US

Award: $10,000 stipend + access to compute and the Pace investment-team network

Duration: 4 weeks (July 2026)

Deadline: Closed 16 May 2026

Four-week paid summer residency for technologists working at the intersection of computing infrastructure, economics and the physical world. Fellows work alongside the Pace investment team and leave with original, publishable research artifacts (one sharp public piece on the systems shaping the next decade). Open to students, researchers, founders and independent thinkers who want real room to go deep on infrastructure and economics questions. Hosted at Pace HQ in NYC.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
